rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the target component or implementation scope, preserving the existing AADL architecture
Place assumptions and guarantees in the component type scope when referring to component ports
Place implementation-level bindings in the implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
Do not use subcomponent ports in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Ensure that assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports are placed in the component type scope
Place implementation-level bindings in the implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
Avoid using subcomponent ports in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are the normal location for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Component implementation annexes are used for implementation-level bindings such as assignments when they are valid in that scope
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports in the implementation scope
Do not place implementation-level bindings in the component type scope
Do not use subcomponent ports in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables chunk 1
